REPLY BRIEF IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION FILED BY PLAINTIFF AGUILA, CHRISTINE G. AGUILA, DARYL

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS CHRISTINE AGUILA, AND, DARYL AGUILA MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION GRANTED, AS MODIFIED. PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION GRANTED THAT REQUIRES DEFENDANT TO KEEP ONE UNIT VACANT FOR PLAINTIFFS AT 1828-1888 GENEVA AVENUE MEETING DESCRIPTION OF "FUTURE HOUSING" IN THE FULL RELEASE AND SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. AS REQUESTED, THE PROPOSED INJUNCTION WAS "MANDATORY." A PRELIMINARY MANDATORY INJUNCTION IS RARELY GRANTED AND IS SUBJECT TO STRICKER REVIEW ON APPEAL. THE GRANTING OF A MANDATORY INJUNCTION IS NOT PERMITTED EXCEPT IN EXTREME CASES WHERE A RIGHT THERE TO IS CLEARLY ESTABLISHED. TEACHERS INS. & ANNUITY ASSN. VS. FURLOTTI (1999) 70 CAL.APP.4TH 1487. PLAINTIFFS ARE LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS AND DEMONSTRATE IRREPARABLE HARM IF THE UNIT IS RENTED OR OTHERWISE BECOMES UNAVAILABLE. PLAINTIFFS HAVE AN INADEQUATE REMEDY BECAUSE THE UNIT IS UNIQUE. THE COURT WAIVES THE BOND REQUIREMENT DUE TO PLAINTIFFS' INDIGENCY. THE COURT ADDS: UNIT MUST BE IDENTIFIED IN THE ORDER. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL WILL PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deft'S Attendance At Deposition And Req. For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-15-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Stephen L. Collier, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Christine G. Aguila
Bruce M. Lubarsky, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Walbridge Terraces, LLC

After hearing, Plaintiffs Christine Aguila and Daryl Aguila's Motion to compel defendants' attendance at deposition and request for monetary sanctions is continued to October 22, 209 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 610. Pending Settlement. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction;
Off Calendar Aug-20-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS CHRISTINE AGUILA, and, DARYL AGUILA Motion for Preliminary Injunction. Appearances: Stephen L. Collier, Esq. Staff Atty. Tenderloin Housing Clinic (415) 771-9850, appearing for Plaintiffs and moving party; Yatin K. Patel, Law Offices of David G. Finkelstein, (650) 353-4503, making a special appearance for Defendants and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for preliminary injunction granted, as modified. Preliminary injunction granted that requires defendant to keep one unit vacant for plaintiffs at 1828-1888 Geneva Avenue meeting description of "future housing" in the Full Release and Settlement Agreement. As requested, the proposed injunction was "mandatory." A preliminary mandatory injunction is rarely granted and is subject to stricker review on appeal. The granting of a mandatory injunction is not permitted except in extreme cases where a right there to is clearly established. Teachers Ins. & Annuity Assn. vs. Furlotti (1999) 70 Cal.App.4th 1487. Plaintiffs are likely to prevail on the merits and demonstrate irreparable harm if the unit is rented or otherwise becomes unavailable. Plaintiffs have an inadequate remedy because the unit is unique. The court waives the bond requirement due to plaintiffs' indigency. The Court adds: Unit must be Identified in the Order. Plaintiff's counsel will prepare Order. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Notice Of Motion For Preference
Off Calendar Jul-23-2009 Granted Per Written Order
